title: hieracium conspurcans subsp. ulothrix
intro: Hieracium conspurcans subsp. ulothrix is a subspecies of the Hieracium conspurcans plant, a flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is also known as mouse-ear hawkweed and is a perennial weed commonly found in Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for respiratory issues and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Hieracium conspurcans subsp. ulothrix include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and skin conditions, and also to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
